title: Right-Censored Remaining-Stint AFT Model
summary: `PitNextLap` is the one-step hazard of an unobserved time-to-next-stop process.
rationale: `PitNextLap` is the one-step hazard of an unobserved time-to-next-stop process. XGBoost’s AFT survival objective directly supports uncensored and right-censored labels, which is a clean way to learn remaining laps until next pit without forcing every row into a noisy binary target.
implementation_hint: For each driver-race sequence, label each row with remaining laps to next pit as `[d,d]`; if no later stop occurs before race end, use right-censoring `[d,+inf)`. Train `xgboost.train` with `objective='survival:aft'`, then convert predicted survival to next-lap hazard `P(T<=1)` or `S(0)-S(1)`. Keep all feature generation strictly past-only within driver-race order and validate by held-out races.
expected_effect: Can learn smoother pit-window timing, especially late in stints and near race end, where binary labels are sparse but the underlying countdown signal is strong.
risk: Converting race-end cases into censored labels needs care because finishing and pitting are different terminal events; a poor conversion can mis-shape late-race hazards.
